description_long: This object is a classic black corded telephone, featuring a rectangular base with a rotary dial on the front face. The handset rests atop the base, with a visibly coiled cord connecting it to the main unit. The rotary dial is circular and metallic, with finger holes for dialing numbers, and is prominently displayed on the front. The handset is ergonomically shaped, with a receiver and mouthpiece at either end, and sits securely in a cradle on the base. The cord is long and flexible, looping outward from the base, suggesting ease of use within a limited range. The overall design is utilitarian and robust, typical of mid-20th-century telephones found in homes and offices.
description: A classic black corded telephone with a rotary dial, coiled cord, and a handset resting on a cradle atop a rectangular base.
description_view_0: Front view showing the handset resting on the base, with the coiled cord extending outward and the rotary dial partially visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the profile of the handset, the cradle, and the full length of the coiled cord extending from the base.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the handset and base, with the coiled cord looping outward and no visible dial.
description_view_3: Opposite side view clearly showing the rotary dial on the front of the base and the handset resting on top.
